acceptance_targets: [chapter_06_F001] Pass if the revised passage spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:241 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:264 contains an on-page cleanup action, an explicit public accusation linked to Iven's promises, and the seizure of at least one key piece of gear happening in scene; fail if the text still jumps from the burst scow to 'It took the rest of the morning' and only reports the confiscation afterward.

[chapter_06_F002] Pass if, after revision, at most one of the cited spans still uses a clipped 'Not X. Not Y.' or 'That was the...' ladder and the remaining spans land in complete sentences with distinct syntax; fail if three or more of those local spans still repeat the same emphasis template.

[FBR-001] Pass if the cited lines read as normal in-world prose with no backticks or code-style formatting, and the betting-board/slate details still remain legible in context. Fail if any inline-code marks remain in those spans.

[FBR-008] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2438-2442 now renders Nami's reaction in chapter-specific physical terms and no "went still" or "went flat" wording remains in that beat. Fail if the replacement is just another generic freeze/blank shorthand.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:241 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:264 contains an on-page cleanup action, an explicit public accusation linked to Iven's promises, and the seizure of at least one key piece of gear happening in scene; fail if the text still jumps from the burst scow to 'It took the rest of the morning' and only reports the confiscation afterward.
findings.problem: After the corpse scow bursts, the chapter time-skips over the hardest labor and over the seizure of the Bitter Thread's working gear. Because the cleanup and confiscation arrive as reported outcome instead of lived scene, the public humiliation loses force at the exact point the chapter's comic escalation should cash out into consequence, and Riva's authority is partly stripped offstage.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the transition anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:241, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:251, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:263 so at least one immediate post-burst beat stays on page: show Iven doing corpse-clearance or recognition labor, show a witness or crew member tying blame to his sold knots, and stage the confiscation of the drag-frame, hawser, or block in front of Riva instead of revealing it later as finished fact.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after revision, at most one of the cited spans still uses a clipped 'Not X. Not Y.' or 'That was the...' ladder and the remaining spans land in complete sentences with distinct syntax; fail if three or more of those local spans still repeat the same emphasis template.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same emphasis engine too often: clipped negation ladders ('Not X. Not Y.') and 'That was the...' setup lines recur across multiple major beats. By the later instances, the rhythm feels templated rather than freshly earned, and some of the fragments drift toward the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the emphasis beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:171, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:235, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:459, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:475 so no more than one of them keeps the current clipped negation-ladder or 'That was the...' structure. Recast the others as complete sentences with different syntactic pressure or image logic, especially in the corpse-jam climax and final authority-shift beat.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the cited lines read as normal in-world prose with no backticks or code-style formatting, and the betting-board/slate details still remain legible in context. Fail if any inline-code marks remain in those spans.
findings.problem: Chapter 6 still contains visible markdown/process artifacts rather than finished prose: the barge name is repeatedly wrapped in backticks, and the betting-board/slate text is formatted as literal inline code. In a shortlist context this reads as uncorrected draft tooling leakage, not an intentional in-world typographic choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Clean the formatting at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2292, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2354, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2394, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2436, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2550, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2654, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2694. Keep the diction, scene rhythm, and the betting-board content, but render the boat name and written text in ordinary narrative typography so the chapter reads as fully authored prose rather than markup residue.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2438-2442 now renders Nami's reaction in chapter-specific physical terms and no "went still" or "went flat" wording remains in that beat. Fail if the replacement is just another generic freeze/blank shorthand.
findings.problem: The same full-book reaction template recurs here: Nami's body "went still," one of seven still/flat beats spread across six chapters. Because other chapters are already carrying this pattern, this instance reads as generic shorthand instead of a local, Nami-specific shock response.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE the reaction at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2438-2442. Keep Nami's fixation on the seed and the market chaos, but swap the stock stillness beat for a bodily response specific to her private chosenness-hunger or sensory narrowing in this moment.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the collision of sanctity, lust, labor, rot, and family resentment. In edge cases, choose the more concrete, uncomfortable, and character-specific rendering over symbolic safety; if an image or action feels too polite for the pressure, it is probably wrong.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, violence, shame, grief, or economic pressure drives a scene, vague language counts as a failed beat; replace euphemism with the exact bodily, social, or material consequence the character experiences.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm across dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for ritual speech, emphasis, or deliberate character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative movement aggressively: follow ordeal with meal, revelation with labor, schism with care, and public spectacle with close-bodied aftermath so no adjacent chapters share the same structural rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, bodily, locally sacred, and unsanitized; lyrical only when pressure earns it, never as a decorative default.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person rotating among the five siblings; each scene stays inside one consciousness and lets the river press through perception rather than omniscient explanation.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate muscular propulsion with denser lyric accumulation; peak scenes should tighten and clarify rather than dissolve into fragmentary haze.
